questions.text: When it comes to fitness and movement — you mentioned daily exercise as a baseline commitment — but what's your philosophy on working out alone versus with others? Are you more of a solo gym-goer who likes to zone out and get it done, do you prefer classes or partner workouts for the accountability and energy, or does it depend on the type of movement you're doing?

Since you're bouncing between Mexico and the US twice a year — do you own any vehicles at all right now, or are you fully relying on walking, ride-shares, and public transit wherever you are? And what's your feeling on car ownership as a digital nomad — is it something you'll eventually want for the freedom, or do you prefer the simplicity of not dealing with a car across borders?

When you're buying things — whether it's gear for work, clothes, household stuff, or gifts for people — do you mostly shop online (Amazon, direct brands) or do you prefer in-store for the instant gratification and ability to see things in person? And are you someone who researches purchases extensively, or do you more of a 'need it, buy it, figure out if it works' person?

What's your relationship with routines and habits outside of work? Do you have specific morning or evening rituals beyond your coffee and wind-down, or is your day-to-day pretty unstructured once the work day ends? I'm thinking about things like daily journaling, specific times you check in with yourself, or anything you do to maintain mental clarity.

When it comes to alcohol and nightlife — are you someone who goes out to bars, clubs, or social drinking events fairly regularly, or is your socializing more centered around other activities? Do you enjoy the bar scene as part of your social life, or do you prefer gatherings in homes, restaurants, or other settings?
